query:
The security reviewer Finding 1 needs to be addressed: Here's the precise reasoning:                                                                
   
  The two pull-formulation variants in power iteration are:                                    
                                                                            
  - Direction::Outgoing pull: node u accumulates from nodes it points to (u→v, so u pulls from 
  v). For edge A→B, A pulls from B. Mass flows backward along edges — predecessors accumulate 
  from seeds. This is reverse/transpose PPR. ✅                                                
  - Direction::Incoming pull: node u accumulates from nodes that point to it (v→u, so u pulls
  from v). For edge A→B, B pulls from A. Mass flows forward along edges — successors accumulate
   from seeds. This is standard forward PPR.
                                                                                               
  ADR-003 specified Direction::Incoming. That mechanism produces standard forward PPR — seeding
   B (decision) propagates mass toward what B supports, not toward A (lesson that supports B).
  The stated goal of surfacing lessons when decisions are seeded requires reverse PPR, which is
   exactly what Direction::Outgoing delivers.                               

  The implementation is behaviorally correct. The four specification artifacts got the         
  mechanism wrong. The doc-comment's "reverse random-walk formulation" is directionally
  accurate but imprecise — it needs to be rewritten to explain exactly why Outgoing achieves   
  the reverse walk.                                                         
\
The distinguishing test for Option B: Construct a minimal graph A→Supports→B, seed           
  exclusively on B. Assert A receives non-trivial PPR score. Then assert that swapping to
  Direction::Incoming causes A to score near-zero (B's incoming neighbor A starts at 0, so A   
  receives no propagated mass). This test cannot pass with both directions — it's the proof
  that Outgoing is the correct choice.

  Action before merge:                                                                         
  - Update ADR-003 (Unimatrix entry #3733) — correct the mechanism, add the Outgoing rationale
  - Update ARCHITECTURE.md, AC-08, R-12 to match                                               
  - Add the distinguishing test to graph_ppr_tests.rs                       
  - Rewrite the doc-comment to be precise: "pull from outgoing targets implements the reverse  
  random walk; Direction::Incoming would implement forward PPR, which propagates away from     
  seeds rather than back to their predecessors"

entries.title: PPR power iteration uses Direction::Outgoing (reverse walk) despite ADR-003 saying Incoming
ADR-003 crt-030: Edge Direction Semantics for PPR Traversal — Outgoing for Reverse/Transpose PPR
Graph algorithm direction semantics: spec describing conceptual traversal vs. iteration variable direction are not the same
ADR-006 crt-034/crt-035: Edge Directionality — v1 One Direction, crt-035 Fulfills Back-fill Contract
PPR regression test trap: inserting only B→A and seeding at B gives A score 0.0 — both edges required
